purpose: Validate identifiers before canonical records are linked. This registry does not rename source-level IDs.

namespaces.note: This namespace type did not previously exist in this registry, though the ch01-is-NNN ID pattern itself was already established and in use by CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own instructorScript record convention, across the four pilot topics (ch01-is-102/103/108/110) and Batch 1 (ch01-is-101/104). Added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to close this gap as part of Batch 1 identifier registration; does not retroactively register the four pilot topics' own instructorScript IDs.

This namespace type did not previously exist in this registry, though the ch01-tNN-block-<type> ID pattern itself was already established and in use by CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own contentBlock record convention, across the four pilot topics and Batch 1. Added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to close this gap as part of Batch 1 identifier registration; does not retroactively register the four pilot topics' own contentBlock IDs.

^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-visual-[0-9]{3}$ was added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to cover the produced visual-asset ID convention already in use by the four pilot topics (ch01-t02-visual-001, ch01-t03-visual-001, ch01-t08-visual-001, ch01-t10-visual-001) and by Batch 1 (ch01-t01-visual-001, ch01-t04-visual-001), which had no matching pattern in this namespace before 1.3.0. This is distinct from ^ch01-vis-[0-9]{3}$, which names visualResolutionId (audit/planning-tracking) records in VISUAL_RESOLUTION_PLAN.json, not produced-asset IDs.

D[0-9]{2} covers duplicateGroups (D01-D05); C[0-9]{2} covers the separate canonicalConflicts series (C01-C04) in the same source file. Both are audit-layer IDs from docs/content-audits/chapter-01/duplicates-and-conflicts.json and are distinguished by prefix letter only.

validationRules: An ID must match exactly one compatible namespace pattern.
A matching pattern is insufficient: the ID must also exist in the namespace authority file unless it is a new canonical design ID.
Locator type and locator namespace must agree.
Inferred cross-source links retain their original IDs and declare linkageType=inferred.
Unknown or ambiguous identifiers set blockingReason to conflictingSource or missingSource.
No source-level identifier is rewritten to fit a canonical namespace.

conflictNamespaceFix.issue: C01-C04 (canonicalConflicts in docs/content-audits/chapter-01/duplicates-and-conflicts.json) matched no registered conflict pattern prior to registryVersion 1.1.0.
conflictNamespaceFix.resolution: Added ^C[0-9]{2}$ as a distinct pattern in the conflict namespace.
conflictNamespaceFix.collisionCheck: Checked ^C[0-9]{2}$ against all other registered patterns in every namespace; no existing ID (SEG*, S2-SEG*, S3-P*, R1-P*, R1-CARD*, S5-P*, ch01-prob-*, S5-OCC*, ch01-sol-*, R1-Q*, S3C-Q*, ch01-quiz-*, V*, R1-VIS-*, S5-VIS-MISSING-*, ch01-vis-*, SCA*, S2-SCI-*, S3-SCI-*, S3C-SCI-*, S5-SCI-*, R1-SCI-*, D[0-9]{2}, S2-DUP-*, S5-DUP-*, S5-CONF-*, CD-CONF-*, ch01-corr-*, ch01-term-*) matches ^C[0-9]{2}$, and C01-C04 match no other pattern. Each real ID now matches exactly one namespace pattern.

batch1NamespaceAdditions.issue: Prior to registryVersion 1.3.0, this registry had no namespace type for instructorScript or contentBlock record IDs at all (despite both being established, in-use CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json record types across the four pilot topics and Batch 1), and its "visual" namespace type had no pattern matching the produced visual-asset ID convention ch01-tNN-visual-NNN (as distinct from ch01-vis-NNN, which names a visualResolutionId planning/audit record, not a produced asset).
batch1NamespaceAdditions.resolution: Added two new namespace types (instructorScript: ^ch01-is-[0-9]{3}$; contentBlock: ^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-block-[a-z]+$) and one new pattern within the existing visual namespace type (^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-visual-[0-9]{3}$), each referencing CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json as authority file, mirroring the existing problem/solution namespace types' own use of that same authority file.
batch1NamespaceAdditions.collisionCheck: Checked all three new patterns against every ID actually present in the approved Batch 1 files (ch01-is-101, ch01-is-104, all twelve ch01-t01-block-*/ch01-t04-block-* blockIds, ch01-prob-104, SRC-CH01-BATCH1-ORIGINAL, ch01-t01-visual-001, ch01-t04-visual-001) and against every other registered namespace pattern. Result: ch01-prob-104 continues to match only the existing problem pattern; SRC-CH01-BATCH1-ORIGINAL continues to match only the existing source pattern; every instructorScript/contentBlock/visual-asset ID now matches exactly one namespace pattern (its own new one) and matched none previously. No existing registered pattern or ID collides with any of the three additions.
batch1NamespaceAdditions.scopeNote: These are schema/namespace-validation additions only. They do not themselves register any specific identifier — see batch1IdentifierRegistration below for the actual Batch 1 registration entries. The four pilot topics' own instructorScript/contentBlock/visual-asset IDs, which already conform to these same newly-added patterns, remain unregistered as individual entries; this task registers Batch 1 identifiers only.

batch1IdentifierRegistration.registeredBy: khubrani2a-gif (project owner), via Claude-executed controlled registration task
batch1IdentifierRegistration.scopeStatement: This registers exactly the Batch 1 content, problem, source-marker, and approved visual identifiers required before a future application-integration step. It does not integrate ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 into apps/chapter1-mvp/, does not modify any approved content or visual file, does not authorize student-facing use, and does not authorize publication. Registration is an internal governance bookkeeping action, not a content-approval or application action.

glossaryTermIdsExcluded.statement: Glossary term IDs are governed inside BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json (glossaryVersion 1.3.0) and are deliberately NOT registered as entries in this file, preserving the established decision that glossary identifiers are glossary-local.

entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (not one of the original seven ContentBlockType values), shared with ch01-t01-block-opening-2 and any future slide record — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note. Was originally registered under the per-slide value "openingConcept" (rev-006-era); ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006 migrated it to the generic "slide" value plus slideNumber (1) and slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr metadata, with no change to blockId or instructional text. Unlike this topic's other six original contentBlock identifiers, this one was not covered by a dedicated separate PHSH111_BATCH1_APPLICATION_INTEGRATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md-style document at registration time; its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.6.0 revisionLog rev-007/rev-008; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.6 revisionLog rev-005/rev-006
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening; was originally registered under the per-slide value "openingConceptSlide2", migrated by ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006 to the generic value plus slideNumber (2) and slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr metadata — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DISTANCE" (an uploaded project-owner slide reference, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by ch01-t01-block-opening's "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1" — neither is registered under the ^SRC-CH01-[A-Z0-9-]+$ namespace pattern, since that pattern is reserved for the chapter's independently audited sources, not project-owner-supplied slide references). A requested Figure Explanation subsection (an uploaded slide figure of racing vehicles using parachutes) was intentionally omitted by explicit project-owner decision — the figure is illustrative and not required for understanding this slide's concept, not an unresolved gap. blocking.blockingReason no longer includes 'missingVisual' (removed by ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006) and visualBaselineDependency remains null; no provisional visual record was created. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0; used as sourceVariants[0].sourceId on ch01-prob-104
entries.notes: Registered strictly as a project-authorship/source-lineage marker. It represents newly authored Batch 1 project content. It is NOT an external audited source, NOT a textbook/slide/review-card/publisher source, and must NOT be treated as independent evidence for any scientific claim. It is distinct from an audited source record (e.g. SRC-CH01-CONV-001, SRC-CH01-PDF-003), a scientific-audit record (e.g. SCA01, S3-SCI-004), and a correction record (e.g. ch01-corr-001, ch01-corr-002) — those substantive scientific records retain their own audited source and correction lineage, entirely independent of this marker.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.8.0 revisionLog rev-010; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.8 revisionLog rev-008
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 3, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional, demonstrating the reusable-slide architecture established by rev-008/ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006. Also introduces generic tableEn/tableAr fields (see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's SourceTable), a mechanism available to any content-block record, used here to reconstruct the project owner's uploaded source table (Physical Quantity / Metric Units / English Units) as a real semantic <table> rather than the screenshot itself. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DISTANCE-UNITS-TABLE" (an uploaded project-owner slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by ch01-t01-block-opening / ch01-t01-block-opening-2). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the uploaded source table was fully available and used, so no visual asset remains missing. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-010/rev-008: pedagogical wording review clarified the Simple Example's unit-consistency sentence, relabeled the dimensions calculation block (now 'Dimensions:' / 'الأبعاد:' with an added no-volume-calculation clarification sentence, avoiding any implied volume computation), and refined the Scientific Note's decimal-multiple/submultiple wording — no change to the original quotation, the source table, the steps, the Table Explanation, the Common Misconception, or the Key Concept.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.9.0 revisionLog rev-011; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.10 revisionLog rev-009/rev-010
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-t01-block-opening-2, and ch01-t01-block-opening-3 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 4, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional. Also introduces generic figureAssetPath/figureAltEn/figureAltAr fields (see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's header note) — a mechanism available to any content-block record, used here to embed the project owner's uploaded coin photograph directly as this slide's figure (no cropping required; the upload was already a clean, tightly-framed image of only the coin and its two measurement labels). visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded figure, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DIFFERENT-UNITS" (an uploaded project-owner slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-3). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the uploaded figure was fully available and used directly. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-010: project-owner Arabic language review refined slideTitleAr and figureAltAr wording (no factual/scientific change; English text unchanged).

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.10.0 revisionLog rev-012; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.12 revisionLog rev-011/rev-012
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-4 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 5, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional. Reuses the generic figureAssetPath/figureAltEn/figureAltAr fields and RAW_FIGURE_URL_BY_BLOCK_ID mechanism introduced for Slide 4 (rev-011) — this time with a hand-authored SVG diagram rather than a raster photograph, demonstrating the mechanism is asset-format-agnostic. The SVG recreates only the rectangle (height 1 ft, width 1.96 ft) from the project-owner-referenced source slide; the source photo's coin fragment (bleeding in from the adjacent, already-integrated Slide 4) and the basketball are deliberately excluded, per explicit project-owner instruction, rather than retained as an unnecessary raster crop. visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded figure, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-AREA-VOLUME" (a project-owner-referenced slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-4). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the rectangle diagram was fully reconstructable from the source slide's own labeled dimensions. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-012: project-owner Arabic scientific-language review corrected three sentences (size-of-a-surface wording changed from 'حجم السطح' to 'مقدار امتداد السطح' to avoid confusion with the physical quantity Volume; Step 1's area/volume derivation bullets revised for grammatical consistency) — no factual/scientific claim changed, English text and figure unchanged.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.11.0 revisionLog rev-013; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.13 revisionLog rev-013
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-5 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 6, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's sixth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the generic tableEn/tableAr source-table mechanism introduced for Slide 3 (rev-009/rev-010), extended in this revision with two new generic, blockType-independent fields (tableCaptionEn/tableCaptionAr — mirroring the slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr convention) so the reconstructed table carries a <caption>, plus a presentation-only, non-slide-specific enhancement to the shared table renderer giving non-null first-column cells (here 'Area (A)'/'Volume (V)') semantic <th scope="rowgroup"> grouping via rowSpan. The reconstructed table uses scientifically formatted superscripts (m², cm², m³, etc.); the verbatim Original English/Arabic quoted prose instead preserves the source's own plain notation (m2, cm2, m3, etc.) unaltered, per explicit project-owner instruction. The short accordion title ('Area and Volume Units' / 'وحدات المساحة والحجم') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded reconstructed table, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-AREA-VOLUME-UNITS-TABLE" (a project-owner-uploaded slide image at /mnt/data/9AD6874A-B410-4B74-8778-4F50F0B1E444.png, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-5). The uploaded screenshot itself is explicitly NOT used as the student-facing visual. blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the table was fully reconstructable from the source slide's own listed units and was used directly. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.13.0 revisionLog rev-015; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.15 revisionLog rev-015
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-6 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 7, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's seventh item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Introduces one new, generic, reusable subsection slot: an optional conversionFactorExplanationMarker on src/features/topics/StructuredSlideContent.tsx's StructuredSlideConfig, a third mutually-exclusive sibling of the existing tableExplanationMarker/figureExplanationMarker slot, for slides with neither a table nor a figure that still need a labeled explanatory subsection there — not slide-7-specific logic. renderSteps() was also generically extended to render a numbered-step clause matching the existing equationBlockPhrase configuration with the same distinct equation-block styling already used for Simple Example paragraphs, so Step 3's clause ('23 m × (3.28084 ft / 1 m)') renders identically to every other equation block in this topic. Lowercase 'm' (the meter unit) was deliberately NOT added to ch01-t01's per-topic equation-italics whitelist in src/content/equationRenderer.tsx — it was already absent, so it already renders upright everywhere in this topic. The conversion factor is rendered as plain inline text with '/' ('3.28084 ft / 1 m'), matching this topic's existing house-style convention for every other ratio/fraction, not a new stacked-fraction mechanism. The short accordion title ('Meters to Feet' / 'التحويل من المتر إلى القدم') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the uploaded source image (/mnt/data/FD0DBBAC-B9AF-4138-A220-563B0F999D48.png) contains only a title and the conversion question, with no instructional figure, so no visual asset is created or referenced by this record, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual'. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-METERS-TO-FEET-CONVERSION", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-6. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-015: a project-owner scientific-precision review corrected Step 5's rounding explanation (no longer presenting 75.5 ft as simultaneously rounded to three significant figures when 23 m itself has only two — now rendering both '23 m ≈ 75.5 ft (rounded to one decimal place)' and '23 m ≈ 75 ft (rounded to two significant figures)' as explicitly labeled results), revised the Simple Example to 'Convert 5.00 m to feet.' / '5.00 m × (3.28084 ft / 1 m) = 16.4042 ft' / '5.00 m ≈ 16.4 ft' with matching significant-figure counts, and revised the Conversion-Factor Explanation so it no longer implies the rounded decimal ratio (3.28084 ft / 1 m) is itself mathematically exact. The equationBlockPhrase config entry in src/features/topics/StructuredSlideContent.tsx was updated to match the revised Simple Example text.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.15.0 revisionLog rev-017; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.17 revisionLog rev-017
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-7 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 8, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's eighth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the generic tableEn/tableAr/tableCaptionEn/tableCaptionAr source-table mechanism introduced for Slide 3/Slide 6 — no schema change needed. The reconstructed table's first column ('Time (t)') groups all three rows under one <th scope="rowgroup" rowSpan={3}> via the same existing null-as-continuation convention. Lowercase 't' (the physical symbol for time) was already present in ch01-t01's per-topic equation-italics whitelist in src/content/equationRenderer.tsx (added for Slide 1's v = d / t), so it already renders italic on this slide with no whitelist change. Lowercase 'h' (the hour unit) was already deliberately excluded from that same whitelist (documented km/h collision), so it already renders upright as a unit symbol here too, with no whitelist change. Lowercase 's' (the second unit) was never whitelisted and continues to render upright. The short accordion title ('Time Measurement' / 'قياس الزمن') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the uploaded source image (/mnt/data/4131B34E-DBB2-45B1-8298-37A5E023B271.png) contains a table with no instructional figure, so no visual asset is created or referenced by this record, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual'. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-TIME-UNITS-TABLE", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-7. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-017: a project-owner scientific-precision review revised the Simple Example's final equivalence line from '1.5 h = 90 min = 5400 s' to '1.5 h = 90 min = 5.4 × 10³ s', adding an explanatory sentence noting that 5.4 × 10³ s makes explicit the two significant figures inherited from the given 1.5 h, while the plain intermediate '5400 s' calculation result is retained unchanged. The equationBlockPhrase config entry in src/features/topics/StructuredSlideContent.tsx was updated to match. No equation-italics whitelist change was made: '10³' uses the same literal Unicode superscript-three character already used elsewhere in this topic (e.g. Slide 6's m², cm³), rendered as plain text with no new token.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.17.0 revisionLog rev-019; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.19 revisionLog rev-019
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-8 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 9, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's ninth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Introduces one new, generic, reusable content mechanism: definitionsEn/definitionsAr fields on ContentBlockRecord (src/types/pilotSchema.ts's new DefinitionEntry shape, mirroring tableEn/tableAr's role), wired through NormalizedSlide.definitions, src/content/adapter.ts, a byte-identical structural-equality check on definitionsEn in src/content/batch1Merge.ts, a new isDefinitionEntryArray() type guard in src/content/validate.ts, and a new renderDefinitionCards() function in src/features/topics/StructuredSlideContent.tsx rendering one <dl> per term with a single <dt>/<dd> pair — not slide-9-specific logic. Also introduces a new optional definitionExplanationMarker on StructuredSlideConfig, a fourth mutually-exclusive sibling of the existing tableExplanationMarker/figureExplanationMarker/conversionFactorExplanationMarker slot. Lowercase 'f' was added to ch01-t01's per-topic equation-italics whitelist in src/content/equationRenderer.tsx after confirming zero prior standalone-word occurrences anywhere in the topic's approved baseline; uppercase 'T' was already present (added for Slide 1's v = d / t). The short accordion title ('Period and Frequency' / 'الزمن الدوري والتردد') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the uploaded source image (/mnt/data/707A6E6A-0F30-4D77-8DC3-B0C3385D32B8.png) contains two definition boxes with no instructional figure requiring raster display, so no visual asset is created or referenced by this record, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual' — the definitions are instead reconstructed as accessible semantic definition cards. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-PERIOD-FREQUENCY-DEFINITIONS", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-8. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-019: a project-owner scientific-precision review revised the Simple Example's final two equations from 'f = 1 / 0.25 s = 4 s⁻¹'/'f = 4 Hz' to 'f = 1 / (0.25 s) = 4.0 s⁻¹'/'f = 4.0 Hz' (parenthesizing the measured denominator and matching the two significant figures of the given 0.25 s period), and revised the Common Misconception's worked correction from 'f = 10 Hz'/'T = 1 / 10 Hz = 0.10 s' to 'f = 10.0 Hz'/'T = 1 / (10.0 Hz) = 0.100 s' for the same reason. The general reciprocal relationships f = 1 / T and T = 1 / f are unchanged. The equationBlockPhrase config entry in src/features/topics/StructuredSlideContent.tsx was updated to match.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.20.0 revisionLog rev-022 (provenance-text-only correction of ch01-t01-block-opening-11's provenanceLinks[0].evidence; supersedes the v1.19.0/rev-021 dependency originally recorded here); ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.21 checksumOnlyCorrections entry ch01-arabic-baseline-checksum-001 (same provenance-text-only correction, sha256 refreshed without a baselineVersion increment per that file's revisionControlPolicy.rule scope)
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-10 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 11, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's eleventh item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the existing generic relationshipExplanationMarker subsection slot introduced for Slide 10 (rev-020) — no new subsection type, table, figure, or definitions mechanism was created. Final typesetting verification (following the project-owner-requested N-italicization correction merged in PR #19, commit 1453b879a0e543721bcd324603b75e6d44f1d24c) confirms f, N, and t render in italic mathematical style, while Δ remains upright. N is italicized only within Slide 11 through a block-scoped additionalItalicTokens configuration on StructuredSlideContent.tsx's STRUCTURED_SLIDE_CONFIG_BY_BLOCK_ID entry for ch01-t01-block-opening-11 and is not added to the topic-wide equation-token whitelist (EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_BY_TOPIC / EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_PROSE_SAFE_BY_TOPIC for ch01-t01 remain exactly ['v','d','t','L','T','M','l','w','V','f'], unchanged, with no 'N' entry). 'Δt' continues to render with 'Δ' upright and 't' italic via the pre-existing 't' whitelist entry and its word-boundary regex; the joined token 'Δt' itself still cannot be matched by the existing \b(...)\b word-boundary whitelist regex (Δ is not a JavaScript \w character), confirmed empirically. Units and unit expressions (s, Hz, cycles/s, s⁻¹) remain upright. Uppercase 'T' and lowercase 'f' remain italic, already whitelisted for ch01-t01 (Slides 1 and 9). The short accordion title ('Stopwatch Frequency' / 'تردد ساعة الإيقاف') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the uploaded source image (/mnt/data/C8A3AD1C-2D89-4511-8B9E-5695BA012929.png) contains a worked problem and equations but no instructional figure requiring raster display, so no visual asset is created or referenced by this record, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual' — the problem and equations are instead reconstructed semantically using the existing equation-rendering architecture. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-STOPWATCH-FREQUENCY-EXAMPLE", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-10. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.21.0 revisionLog rev-023; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.22 revisionLog rev-022
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-11 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 12, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's twelfth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the existing generic tableExplanationMarker subsection slot (the same one Slides 3/6/8 use) — no new subsection type was created. Two new, generic, reusable StructuredSlideConfig fields were added to StructuredSlideContent.tsx to resolve a genuine token-collision this slide introduces: equationPhraseItalicTokens (italicizes a bare letter only within one exact, verified equation substring, e.g. 'a' in 'a = F_net / m' and 'g' in 'W = m g', leaving every other occurrence of that letter — including the English article 'a' and the gram unit 'g' — completely untouched) and equationAwareSections (opts Common Misconception and Scientific Note into the same equation-block styling Steps/Simple Example already always receive, since those two subsections state their own equations here). Both fields default to unset/empty for every other existing slide's config, verified by the full 749-test suite passing unchanged immediately after the architecture change landed, before this slide's own content was added — neither field changes any other slide's rendering. 'm' (mass), 'F' (fused only as 'F_net'), and 'W' (weight) are italicized via the existing blockId-scoped additionalItalicTokens mechanism (the same one Slide 11 used for 'N'), verified collision-free — 'm' in particular does not collide with a meters-unit abbreviation anywhere in Slide 12, unlike ch01-t04's excluded 'm'. None of 'm'/'F'/'W'/'a'/'g' was added to EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_BY_TOPIC or EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_PROSE_SAFE_BY_TOPIC in src/content/equationRenderer.tsx (both remain exactly ['v','d','t','L','T','M','l','w','V','f'] for ch01-t01). The short accordion title ('Mass and Inertia' / 'الكتلة والقصور الذاتي') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the uploaded source image (/mnt/data/4185AF1B-7E9B-4616-AA8C-AB745EEBE85D.png) contains explanatory text and a table but no instructional figure requiring raster display, so no visual asset is created or referenced by this record, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual' — the table is instead reconstructed semantically as a real <table> reusing the existing generic tableEn/tableAr mechanism. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-MASS-INERTIA-TABLE", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-11. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.22.0 revisionLog rev-024; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.23 revisionLog rev-023
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-12 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 13, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's thirteenth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the existing generic figureExplanationMarker subsection slot (the same one Slide 4 uses) — no new subsection type was created. Reuses Slide 12's equationPhraseItalicTokens and equationAwareSections mechanisms unmodified, and adds one new, generic, reusable StructuredSlideConfig field, originalEmphasisPhrases, which wraps one exact, verified substring within the Original English/Arabic subsection in a semantic <strong> — used here to preserve the source's own emphasis on 'not' (English) / 'ليست' (Arabic) in 'but is not the same as mass' without inserting any markup into the approved verbatim text itself. This field defaults to unset for every other existing slide's config, verified by the full 810-test suite passing unchanged immediately after the architecture change landed, before this slide's own content was added — it changes no other slide's rendering. 'F' (fused only as 'F_net'), 'W', and 'g' are italicized via the existing blockId-scoped additionalItalicTokens mechanism — 'g' is safely blockId-wide here (unlike Slide 12) since Slide 13 never states a mass in grams, so there is no gram-unit collision to guard against. 'm' and 'a' are NOT blockId-wide safe: 'm' collides with the metres-per-second-squared unit abbreviation used in this slide's own equations ('9.8 m/s²', '2.0 m/s²'), and 'a' is the English indefinite article recurring throughout this slide's own prose — both are instead italicized only within the exact equation substrings where each is a genuine variable, via equationPhraseItalicTokens. Slide 13 does not inherit Slide 11's separate blockId-scoped 'N' entry — additionalItalicTokens is scoped per blockId, and 'N' never appears italic in Slide 13 (it is only the newton force unit there, in '196 N' and '40 N'). None of 'm'/'F'/'W'/'a'/'g'/'N' was added to EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_BY_TOPIC or EQUATION_ITALIC_TOKENS_PROSE_SAFE_BY_TOPIC in src/content/equationRenderer.tsx (both remain exactly ['v','d','t','L','T','M','l','w','V','f'] for ch01-t01). The short accordion title ('Mass versus Weight' / 'الكتلة مقابل الوزن') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null: the figure asset (docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-visuals/ch01-t01-block-opening-13-figure.jpg) is a Batch-1-drafting-scope figure resolved via figureAssetPath/RAW_FIGURE_URL_BY_BLOCK_ID (the same generic mechanism Slide 4 uses), not a separate visual-governance-tracked asset, and blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual' now that this figure is integrated. The source illustration (already focused to the two-panel lifting/pulling drawing, excluding the source slide's green title banner, bullet text, and white margins) was supplied by the project owner directly as a chat attachment and used as this figure asset without further cropping. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-MASS-WEIGHT-SUITCASE", following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-12. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

unchangedByThisRegistration: No approved English or Arabic topic JSON file was modified; both remain byte-identical to their baseline-approval checksums.
Neither approved SVG nor either visual-validation record was modified; both remain byte-identical to VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json's recorded checksums.
BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json remains unchanged, still glossaryVersion 1.3.0; no glossary term ID was registered here.
No route, import, or registry entry was added anywhere in apps/chapter1-mvp/.
applicationIntegrationAuthorized remains false for both topics.
studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere.

registrationHistory.scope: Batch 1 identifier registration (ch01-t01, ch01-t04): registered the 18 identifiers required before application integration (2 instructorScript, 12 contentBlock, 1 problem, 1 source-authorship marker, 2 visual assets), and added the instructorScript and contentBlock namespace types plus the ch01-tNN-visual-NNN pattern within the existing visual namespace type, all previously missing from this registry despite being already-established CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json ID conventions in use by the four pilot topics and Batch 1 alike.
registrationHistory.applicationAndPublicationRestrictions: This registration does not authorize application integration, student-facing use, or publication for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04. applicationIntegrationAuthorized, studentFacingAllowed, and studentPublicationAuthorized all remain false.
registrationHistory.projectOwnerDecisionBasis: Project-owner explicit request to register the approved Batch 1 identifiers required before application integration, following the same controlled-registration pattern implied by the existing conflict-namespace precedent (registryVersion 1.1.0) and by each topic's own identifierRegistrationStatus.pending notes in ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, and VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json.
